response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior software engineer (10+ years) with strong full-stack credentials, but the role specifically demands a C++ developer with deep STL, multi-threading, message brokers, IEC 62443/SDL, and industrial automation exposure. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (Dec 2014 – Jun 2015) with the bulk of the career in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/cloud work. No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA, or on-premises industrial platforms. Skill alignment is too low for a Mid-Senior C++ architect role.
strengths: 10+ years of senior-level software engineering with architecture and mentoring experience
Strong systems/low-latency background (DLang microservices handling millions of req/s, async I/O, fibers)
Solid agile/TDD/CI-CD craftsmanship and public technical communication (talks, workshops)
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL experience — only ~6 months at National Instruments back in 2014–2015
No demonstrated exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity practices
No evidence of MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, message brokers, or industrial automation/IoT platforms

key_factors.factor: Strong general senior engineering & systems experience
